context: how do I install a package in ubuntu?
reference: use sudo apt-get install packagename or sudo apt install packagename

context: how to install python3 on ubuntu
reference: run sudo apt install python3 in terminal

context: how to install java on ubuntu [SEP] I need java for my application
reference: run sudo apt install default-jdk to install java development kit

context: how to install nodejs on ubuntu 22.04
reference: run sudo apt install nodejs npm or use nvm for version management

context: how to install git on ubuntu
reference: sudo apt install git then configure with git config --global user.name and user.email

context: how to update ubuntu packages
reference: run sudo apt update to refresh package list then sudo apt upgrade to install updates

context: how to upgrade ubuntu to new version
reference: run sudo do-release-upgrade in terminal to upgrade to the next ubuntu release

context: ubuntu says package is held back [SEP] apt upgrade not installing everything
reference: use sudo apt full-upgrade or sudo apt-get dist-upgrade to install held back packages

context: how to remove a package in ubuntu
reference: use sudo apt remove packagename to remove or sudo apt purge packagename to remove with config files

context: how to fix broken packages ubuntu [SEP] apt install fails with broken packages error
reference: run sudo apt --fix-broken install to resolve broken package dependencies

context: how to list installed packages ubuntu
reference: use dpkg --list or apt list --installed to see all installed packages

context: how to search for a package in ubuntu
reference: use apt search keyword or apt-cache search keyword to find available packages

context: how to check disk space ubuntu
reference: use df -h to see disk usage in human readable format or du -sh folder to check folder size

context: how to mount a usb drive in ubuntu
reference: ubuntu usually automounts usb drives but you can manually mount with sudo mount /dev/sdb1 /mnt/usb

context: how to change file permissions in linux
reference: use chmod command for example chmod 755 file.sh or chmod +x file.sh to make executable

context: how to find a file in ubuntu terminal
reference: use find / -name filename to search entire system or find . -name filename to search current directory

context: how to create a symbolic link in linux
reference: use ln -s /path/to/original /path/to/link to create a symbolic link

context: how to check ip address ubuntu
reference: use ip addr show or hostname -I to display your ip address

context: wifi not working ubuntu [SEP] wireless network not connecting
reference: try sudo service network-manager restart or check drivers with lspci -k | grep -A 3 Network

context: how to setup static ip ubuntu
reference: edit /etc/netplan/01-netcfg.yaml and set addresses dhcp4 no then run sudo netplan apply

context: how to check open ports ubuntu
reference: use sudo netstat -tulpn or sudo ss -tulpn to list all open ports and listening services

context: how to enable firewall ubuntu
reference: use sudo ufw enable to enable firewall and sudo ufw allow 22 to allow ssh port

context: how to check cpu usage ubuntu
reference: use top or htop command to monitor cpu and memory usage in real time

context: how to kill a process in ubuntu
reference: use kill PID to send sigterm or kill -9 PID to force kill a process use ps aux to find the PID

context: how to check ubuntu version
reference: run lsb_release -a or cat /etc/os-release to see the ubuntu version

context: how to check ram usage ubuntu
reference: use free -h to see memory usage in human readable format

context: ubuntu running slow [SEP] system became very slow after update
reference: check cpu and memory usage with top and run sudo apt autoremove to free up space

context: how to start a service in ubuntu
reference: use sudo systemctl start servicename and sudo systemctl enable servicename to start on boot

context: how to check if apache is running
reference: run sudo systemctl status apache2 to see if apache web server is running

context: how to restart nginx ubuntu
reference: use sudo systemctl restart nginx to restart the nginx web server

context: how to view service logs ubuntu
reference: use sudo journalctl -u servicename to view logs or sudo journalctl -xe for recent errors

context: how to enable ssh on ubuntu
reference: install with sudo apt install openssh-server then sudo systemctl enable ssh and sudo systemctl start ssh

context: how to connect to server via ssh
reference: use ssh username@hostname or ssh username@ip_address to connect to remote server

context: ssh permission denied ubuntu [SEP] cannot login via ssh
reference: check ssh key permissions with chmod 600 ~/.ssh/id_rsa and ensure authorized_keys file exists on server

context: how to copy files over ssh ubuntu
reference: use scp localfile username@server:/remote/path to copy files securely over ssh

context: how to add user in ubuntu
reference: use sudo adduser username to create a new user interactively

context: how to give sudo access to user ubuntu
reference: run sudo usermod -aG sudo username to add user to sudo group

context: how to change password ubuntu
reference: use passwd command to change your own password or sudo passwd username to change another users password

context: how to switch user in ubuntu terminal
reference: use su - username to switch to another user or sudo -i to switch to root

context: how to install gnome on ubuntu
reference: run sudo apt install ubuntu-desktop to install gnome desktop environment

context: screen resolution wrong ubuntu [SEP] cannot change display resolution
reference: open settings display or use xrandr command to set resolution for example xrandr --output HDMI-1 --mode 1920x1080

context: how to take screenshot ubuntu
reference: press prtscn key for full screenshot or use gnome-screenshot -a for area selection

context: how to run a bash script ubuntu
reference: make it executable with chmod +x script.sh then run it with ./script.sh

context: how to set environment variable linux
reference: use export VARNAME=value in terminal or add to ~/.bashrc for permanent setting

context: how to add to PATH in ubuntu
reference: add export PATH=$PATH:/your/directory to ~/.bashrc file then run source ~/.bashrc

context: how to redirect output to file linux
reference: use command > file.txt to overwrite or command >> file.txt to append output to file

context: how to grep search text in files linux
reference: use grep pattern filename or grep -r pattern directory to search recursively

context: how to install nvidia drivers ubuntu
reference: run ubuntu-drivers autoinstall or sudo apt install nvidia-driver-535 then reboot

context: printer not working ubuntu [SEP] cannot print from ubuntu
reference: open cups interface at localhost:631 in browser or install printer with sudo apt install cups

context: no sound ubuntu [SEP] audio not working after install
reference: run pulseaudio --start or alsamixer in terminal to check audio levels and unmute channels

context: ubuntu not booting [SEP] black screen on startup
reference: try booting with nomodeset kernel parameter by pressing e in grub menu and adding nomodeset before quiet splash

context: how to fix grub after windows install
reference: boot from ubuntu live usb then run sudo grub-install and sudo update-grub to restore grub bootloader

context: how to change default boot entry grub ubuntu
reference: edit /etc/default/grub and change GRUB_DEFAULT value then run sudo update-grub

context: how to check system logs ubuntu
reference: use sudo journalctl to view systemd logs or check /var/log/syslog for system messages

context: how to check boot errors ubuntu
reference: run sudo journalctl -b to see logs from current boot or dmesg to see kernel messages

context: ubuntu crash after update [SEP] system keeps crashing
reference: check crash reports in /var/crash directory or run apport-cli to analyze crash dump

context: how to create virtual environment python ubuntu
reference: use python3 -m venv myenv to create then source myenv/bin/activate to activate it

context: pip install permission denied ubuntu
reference: use pip install --user packagename to install for current user only or use virtual environment

context: how to install docker ubuntu
reference: add docker repository with curl script from docs.docker.com then sudo apt install docker-ce

context: how to run python script as service ubuntu
reference: create a systemd service file in /etc/systemd/system/ with ExecStart pointing to your python script
